$comment: Schema descriptor for .yengo-ingest.sqlite — per-external-source backend-only SQLite ingest state DB. Source of truth: backend/puzzle_manager/core/source_ingest_db.py (_SCHEMA_SQL_V1). Last Updated: 2026-05-03
title: .yengo-ingest.sqlite Schema
description: Per-source ingest state database. Co-located inside each external source directory at <source.path>/.yengo-ingest.sqlite. Owns content-aware skip, resume, and rename detection for one source. Replaces the legacy positional AdapterCheckpoint JSON. Never shipped to browser. See docs/architecture/backend/source-ingest-db.md.

tables.name: meta
tables.description: Key-value table for database-level metadata: schema version, format identifier, source binding, and run history.

columns.name: value
columns.description: Metadata value as a string. Integers and timestamps are encoded as text and parsed at read time.

required_keys.description: Format identifier. Always 'yengo-source-ingest' for this database.
The source.id value from sources.json that this database belongs to. Validated on open.
run_id of the first run that created this database. Acts as migrator-completion marker.
run_id of the most recent run that touched this database. Updated on close.
ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent run.

tables.name: files
tables.description: One row per source file ever observed. Acts as the canonical ingest checkpoint: presence + matching content_hash means 'already ingested'. No separate checkpoint table exists; rows are the checkpoint.

columns.name: rel_path
columns.description: POSIX path relative to the source root (e.g. 'capture-problems/0001.sgf'). Always forward slashes; never absolute.

columns.name: content_hash
columns.description: 16-char lowercase hex. SHA-256(raw_sgf_bytes)[:16]. Computed via core.naming.generate_content_hash. Same hash used for GN, filename, and yengo-content.db.content_hash.

columns.name: size_bytes
columns.description: File size in bytes at last observation. Cheap stat-based change-detection signal.

columns.name: mtime_ns
columns.description: File mtime in nanoseconds at last observation. Cheap stat-based change-detection signal. Not trusted alone; v1 always re-hashes (Tier-3 policy).

columns.name: status
columns.description: 0=ingested (success), 1=skipped (intentional, e.g. wrong size), 2=failed (validation error or duplicate). Stored as integer for compactness.

columns.name: skip_reason
columns.description: Nullable. Diagnostic string when status != 0 (e.g. 'invalid_sgf', 'duplicate_position', 'no_solution_tree'). Free-form.

columns.name: run_id
columns.description: run_id of the run that last touched this row. Used for rename-tiebreak (most-recent wins) and forensics.

indexes.name: idx_files_hash
indexes.description: Rename-detection lookup and cross-reference to yengo-content.db.

indexes.name: idx_files_status
indexes.description: Sub-millisecond progress queries via SELECT status, COUNT(*) GROUP BY status.

indexes.name: idx_files_run
indexes.description: Forensic queries: 'what did this run touch?'

skip_logic.description: Tier-3 (correctness-first) skip policy. For each walked file: stat for (size, mtime); if matches stored row, ALWAYS re-read and recompute content_hash to confirm identity. Only skip downstream stages if recomputed hash matches stored value. This eliminates platform-dependent mtime precision concerns.

rename_detection.description: When a walked file's rel_path is not in `files`, look up by content_hash. If a row exists for an old rel_path that no longer exists on disk, update the row's rel_path (treat as rename, no re-ingest). Tiebreak on multiple matches: highest (most-recent) run_id wins.

see_also.description: Full design document: rationale, lifecycle, deletions enabled, phased plan.
yengo-content.db schema — backend cross-source content store. Same content_hash bridges the two.
The three databases (search, content, source-ingest) and their boundaries.
Python implementation: schema DDL, SourceIngestDB class, migrator chain.
